page: UpCloud GPU-48xCPU-480GB-2xB200 is a GPU-accelerated server instance designed for intensive parallel computing workloads. It features two NVIDIA Blackwell B200 GPUs providing a total of 384 GB of VRAM. The host system is equipped with 48 vCPUs powered by Intel Xeon 8570 processors running at 2.0 GHz on the x86_64 architecture, supported by 480.0 GB of system memory. This configuration yields a memory-to-core ratio of 10.0 GB per core.

Benchmark data indicates strong multi-core and memory subsystem performance. The instance achieved a PassMark CPU score of 88533.65 and a stress-ng multi-core CPU performance score of 97240.84, both placing in the top tier and strong categories respectively. Memory bandwidth is highly efficient, with read-only small block cached speeds reaching 7105.96 GB/sec and large block uncached read speeds at 385.14 GB/sec. For database operations, the server demonstrated 117.34 million operations per second in Redis SET benchmarks and a PassMark database score of 33288.64. Large language model inference on a small 135M model yielded 9458.91 tokens per second for prompt processing.

In terms of resource allocation, the CPU cores are designated as shared, which may introduce performance variability compared to dedicated instances. Additionally, the instance does not include local storage, meaning users must provision external block storage for persistent data. From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the high density of GPU VRAM and system memory relative to the vCPU count positions this server as a specialized resource for workloads requiring massive memory footprints and parallel acceleration, rather than general-purpose compute.

This server is suited for large-scale artificial intelligence workloads, including LLM inference, deep learning model training, high-performance computing simulations, and memory-intensive database operations.
description: UpCloud GPU-48xCPU-480GB-2xB200 is a high-capacity GPU-accelerated server configured with two NVIDIA Blackwell B200 GPUs delivering 384 GB of combined VRAM. It features 48 vCPUs on an Intel Xeon 8570 processor architecture running at 2.0 GHz, paired with 480.0 GB of system memory. Benchmark results show top-tier performance in multi-core processing, memory bandwidth, and database operations, with Redis SET performance reaching 117.34 million operations per second. However, the instance relies on shared CPU allocation and lacks local storage, requiring external storage solutions. This server is qualitatively cost-effective for specialized parallel computing tasks rather than standard compute. It is designed for workloads such as large language model inference, deep learning, high-performance computing, and memory-intensive database applications.
og_description: UpCloud GPU-48xCPU-480GB-2xB200 is a GPU-accelerated server featuring two NVIDIA Blackwell B200 GPUs, 48 vCPUs, and 480 GB RAM, optimized for parallel computing and large-scale AI inference.

tagline: A high-capacity parallel computing platform featuring dual next-generation GPUs and extensive system memory for demanding artificial intelligence workloads.
bullet_points: Dual NVIDIA Blackwell B200 GPUs with 384 GB total VRAM
48 Intel Xeon vCPUs with 480 GB of system memory
Top-tier memory bandwidth and multi-core decompression performance
Shared CPU allocation and no local storage included
Optimized for LLM inference, deep learning, and HPC workloads
